title: Venezuela earthquake: 32 killed, over 700 injured; state of emergency declared
content: In the wake of devastating twin earthquakes, Venezuela has declared a national state of emergency. Infrastructure has been severely impacted, with many buildings reduced to rubble and fears of casualties potentially soaring between 10,000 and 100,000, according to the USGS. Rescue operations have been hindered, especially in the capital Caracas and at Maiquetia International Airport, where damage is extensive.

title: 'Not about him': Rahul Gandhi expresses regret in defamation case by Shivraj Singh's son
content: Rahul Gandhi has approached the Madhya Pradesh High Court, expressing regret over a statement allegedly defaming Kartikeya Singh, son of Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan. Gandhi's lawyer submitted an application stating the remark was unrelated to Singh. Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint in a Bhopal court, leading to a summons for Gandhi. The Congress leader is now seeking to quash these proceedings.

title: Neymar matches Pelé with one of Brazil's rarest World Cup feat
content: Neymar made his long-awaited World Cup return in Brazil’s 3-0 win over Scotland, becoming only the fourth Brazilian to appear at four World Cups and just the second after Pelé to wear Brazil’s iconic No. 10 shirt across four editions. The 34-year-old impressed in a brief cameo as Brazil finished top of Group C and advanced unbeaten to the Round of 32.

title: Google engineer reportedly sacked over viral Workspace CLI AI tool
content: Ex-Google engineer Justin Poehnelt says he was fired after his viral Google Workspace CLI hit No. 1 on Hacker News, drawing thousands of GitHub stars and users in days. The seven-year veteran claims Workspace leaders feared disruption from AI agents, not his tool itself. The irony: Google announced an official Workspace CLI at Cloud Next just two days before his termination. His X thread has since crossed millions of views.

title: 2,004 calls, 238 hours & one final 'signal': How Pune businessman's murder unfolded
content: A chilling conspiracy has emerged in the death of real estate businessman Ketan Agarwal, allegedly orchestrated by his fiancée Siya Goyal and her lover Chetan Chaudhary. Investigators revealed months of secret communication and repeated attempts to lure Agarwal to Lohagad Fort before his fatal fall. CCTV footage and phone records point to a meticulously planned murder, with earlier attempts reportedly failing.

title: Japan’s second richest man SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son junks his plan to retire in sixties
content: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son, 68, plans to lead the tech giant for another decade, focusing intensely on artificial intelligence and robotics.  Dismissing 'AI bubble' fears as 'blasphemy,' Son believes AI's potential is just unfolding.  He aims to achieve 'artificial superintelligence' and is backing this vision with significant investments in companies like Arm and OpenAI, alongside robotics acquisitions.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents arrive in Belfast ahead of potential India debut
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the youngest ever selected for India's men's team, is poised for a potential international debut against Ireland. His rapid rise, marked by a record-breaking List A fifty and a stellar IPL 2026 season, has generated immense excitement. With his parents present in Belfast, fans eagerly await his first cap, a dream fulfilled for the 15-year-old prodigy.

title: Embracing grey hair may reveal your mindset, self-confidence, and emotional maturity
content: Discovering your first grey hair often sparks a deeper psychological journey.  Research reveals women embracing their natural grey are pushing back against societal stigma, prioritizing authenticity over appearing youthful. This choice signifies a quiet act of self-acceptance and emotional maturity, demonstrating a shift in self-worth away from external validation and towards inner peace.  It's a powerful statement of embracing who you are.

title: Anthropic's engineering leader admits that coding with Claude Code 'hurt' developers
content: AI coding tools like Claude Code are making software development a solitary pursuit, according to Anthropic's Fiona Fung. Engineers are spending more time with AI, leading to a decline in team collaboration. To combat this isolation, Anthropic is fostering in-person interaction through programming lunches and shared 'maker time,' enabling developers to learn from each other's unique AI workflows and maintain crucial teamwork.

title: NBA trade rumor: Lakers eye Cavaliers center to support Luka Doncic
content: The Los Angeles Lakers would reportedly pursue a sign-and-trade sending LeBron James to the Cleveland Cavaliers for center Jarrett Allen, ESPN's Brian Windhorst said June 24, 2026 on ESPN Cleveland radio. James, an unrestricted free agent carrying a roughly $59.5 million cap hold, made $52.6 million last season. The proposal has gained traction because Allen would address the Lakers' long-standing need at center while complementing Luka Doncic and newly re-signed Austin Reaves.

title: ‘Felt humiliated’: How row over train door turns deadly in Mumbai local; passenger stabbed
content: A shocking act of violence unfolded on a Mumbai local train as a clothing store employee allegedly stabbed a co-passenger to death following an argument over a train door. The dispute, reportedly sparked by rain splashing passengers, escalated when the accused, feeling humiliated, repeatedly attacked the victim. Authorities apprehended the suspect in Panvel while he was attempting to flee.

title: Watch: Venezuela, Japan & California shaken by powerful quakes within hours
content: A day of powerful seismic activity saw major earthquakes strike Venezuela, Japan, and California. Venezuela experienced the most devastating impact with twin quakes causing fatalities and building collapses. Japan's northeast coast was hit by a strong tremor, while northern California also registered a significant quake. Experts confirm these events are unrelated, occurring on separate fault lines.

title: Missiles, drones, damaged bases and depleted stockpiles: Trump faces massive Iran war bill
content: US military actions against Iran have incurred significant costs, estimated between $34 billion and $42 billion by CSIS. Congress now faces the challenge of funding these unbudgeted expenses, with the Pentagon preferring supplemental appropriations to protect modernization programs. The conflict's financial toll includes substantial spending on munitions, equipment losses, and base damage, with broader economic impacts also noted.

title: NCERT flags circulation of pirated textbooks, fake class IX social science book
content: National Council of Educational Research and Training NCERT has cautioned against circulation of pirated and unauthorised textbooks in print and digital formats across online platforms in India. A fake Class IX Social Science textbook titled Understanding Society India Beyond is being shared on social media, websites and groups. NCERT has advised stakeholders to rely only on official sources including its website ePathshala and authorised vendors, while announcing legal action under Copyright Act 1957 against offenders.

title: ‘Had drinks’: Karnataka tourist ignores warning, swept away while sitting on rocks at Goa beach
content: A tourist's holiday in Goa turned tragic after he ignored safety warnings and his friend's pleas, losing his life at Baga beach. Ashpaq Masali, 34, reportedly consumed alcohol before attempting a daredevil stunt on slippery rocks, only to be swept away by a powerful wave. Despite rescue efforts, his body was later recovered. Authorities had previously advised against venturing near the sea during the monsoon due to hazardous conditions.

title: Trump kicks off ‘Great State Fair’: What America’s 250th anniversary will look like
content: President Trump inaugurated a 16-day celebration for America's 250th anniversary on the National Mall, featuring a "Great American State Fair" with diverse displays and performances. The event promises the world's largest fireworks display on July 4th, alongside athletic competitions and a new Ferris wheel. Despite some performer withdrawals and political sensitivities, the festivities aim to showcase national pride and a revitalized America.

title: Why every homeowner needs a Khata certificate: process, documents & benefits explained
content: Securing a Khata certificate is crucial for all property owners in Karnataka, validating ownership and enabling essential services. This municipal document is mandatory for property tax payments, bank loans, utility connections, and building permits. The process involves submitting required documents and a physical property verification. Obtaining a Khata ensures your property's legal standing and smooth transactions within the civic system.

title: Delhi horror: After 10-year-old's rape, murder, accused cleaned cab, took ride
content: A habitual offender, Basu Singh, allegedly abducted, raped, and murdered a 10-year-old girl before attempting to meticulously erase evidence.  He changed clothes and scrubbed his cab, then resumed his usual work.  Singh, with a history of five cases including molestation and attempted murder, claimed family land disputes were behind the charges. Police apprehended him after technical surveillance.

title: Dustin Poirier opens up on airport arrest and life after UFC retirement
content: Dustin Poirier has admitted he needs help after video of his Atlanta airport arrest was released by the Atlanta Police Department. The retired UFC star said adjusting to life after fighting has been difficult and acknowledged that alcohol is not helping. In an emotional Instagram message, Poirier said he wants to protect his family and take the right steps to improve his life moving forward.

title: Josh Hokit stands by Michelle Obama remark despite White, Cormier backlash
content: Josh Hokit has defended his controversial Michelle Obama remark, saying he believed it was a compliment and an example of free speech. His explanation has not reduced criticism, with figures such as Dana White and Daniel Cormier strongly condemning the comment. Hokit, however, continues to stand by his words and has refused to apologize.

title: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo face off in football’s net worth race
content: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have spent nearly two decades battling for football supremacy. This time, the contest is about money. Both legends have crossed the billionaire mark and stand among the richest athletes in the world. Their fortunes have surged through football contracts, sponsorships, business ventures, and social media influence. While Ronaldo is believed to hold a slight edge in total wealth, Messi is not far behind in this remarkable financial race.

title: Can college withhold certificates for unpaid course fee? Calcutta HC says no
content: A dental college must refund a student's full fees and return her original documents after she withdrew from an MDS program within six days. The Calcutta High Court ruled the institution violated UGC guidelines by refusing to return the Rs 9.5 lakh and holding onto her certificates, deeming the practice void as against public policy. The court emphasized the statutory force of UGC directives for all higher education institutions.

title: Is Cricket more global than Football? One has more people, the other more countries
content: As the Cricket vs Football debate resurfaces during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, this analysis argues that population figures alone don't determine a sport's global reach. While cricket's tournament nations represent more people, much of that comes from India and Pakistan. Football's strength lies in its wider geographic spread, while cricket enjoys deeper popularity in a smaller number of densely populated countries.

title: 'He slipped while ... ': How inconsistent statements exposed Siya-Chetan's murder plot
content: In a chilling incident at Lohagad Fort, a real estate director was reportedly pushed off a cliff by his fiancée's partner. Investigators discovered that the crime was carefully orchestrated, with the fiancée acting as the signaler for the attack. The investigation gained momentum from CCTV evidence and conflicting testimonies from her, prompting arrests.

title: RVUNL recruitment: Short notice for 2,005 JE, JA and Accountant vacancies released
content: RVUNL Recruitment 2026: Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Utpadan Nigam Limited (RVUNL) has announced a recruitment drive for 2,005 vacancies across five Rajasthan power companies. The openings include Junior Engineer-I, Junior Accountant and Junior Assistant/Commercial Assistant-II posts. A detailed notification and application schedule will be released in the second fortnight of July 2026. Eligible candidates aged 18 to 28 years can apply online. The selection process includes a written examination, document verification and medical examination.

title: NCERT introduces section on Emergency in Class IX textbook for first time
content: For the first time, NCERT has introduced a chapter on the Emergency from 1975-77 in the Class IX curriculum. This segment sheds light on the suspension of fundamental rights and censorship, underscoring the crucial challenges faced by Indian democracy. The chapter, titled 'Understanding Society: India and Beyond,' also recognizes Jayaprakash Narayan's influence and the subsequent elections, celebrating democracy's resilience after 51 years since the Emergency was declared.

title: 19 fours, 6 sixes! Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's younger brother Ashirvad hits blistering century
content: Young cricketer Ashirvad Sooryavanshi, just 10 years old, is making waves with his exceptional batting.  He recently smashed a stunning 168 off 119 balls in a practice match, following a debut century.  Despite only six months of playing, his aggressive style and big scores are drawing attention, even from his brother Vaibhav, who is currently with the Indian team.

title: Watch: Roads split, buildings crumble as two quakes in 39 seconds jolt Venezuela
content: Venezuela experienced a shocking dual earthquake event, registering magnitudes of 7.2 and 7.5, striking near San Felipe and Yumare. The tremors led to extensive damage including collapsed buildings and disrupted transport, particularly affecting multiple states and the capital, Caracas. Video footage captured the chaos as residents evacuated swaying structures amidst fears of impending aftershocks.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for June 25, 2026 - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: Olectra Greentech, GAIL, and Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd (HAL) have been picked as the top 3 stocks to buy today on June 25, 2026 by Aakash K Hindocha, Vice President - Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group/Nuvama Wealth Management.

title: Neymar returns, Vinícius scores twice as Brazil cruise into FIFA World Cup knockouts
content: Brazil sealed top spot in Group C with a dominant 3-0 win over Scotland at the 2026 World Cup, led by Vinícius Júnior’s first-half brace and Matheus Cunha’s strike. Neymar returned from injury as a substitute. Scotland’s spirited effort fell short, leaving them third and dependent on results to reach the knockout stage as a best third-placed team overall campaign.

title: Nirjala Ekadashi: Date, parana time, puja vidhi, mantra and significance
content: Hindus observe Nirjala Ekadashi on June 25, 2026, a highly auspicious day dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This strict fast, observed without food or water until the next day, is believed to bestow the benefits of all 24 Ekadashis and cleanse past sins. Devotees engage in prayers, donations, and chanting, with the fast breaking on Dwadashi after specific rituals. Health precautions are advised due to the summer observance.

title: Who wants to be a millionaire? Contestant lands life-changing sum after simple World Cup question
content: A contestant on Who Wants To Be A Millionaire? won £64,000 after correctly answering a FIFA World Cup question about which continent has hosted the tournament most often. Using his knowledge of football history, he identified Europe as the correct answer and secured one of the night's biggest prizes. The story highlights how World Cup trivia can prove lucrative, or costly under pressure.

title: Crude slips toward $70 as tankers return to Hormuz after US-Iran reach peace deal
content: Crude oil prices have plummeted towards pre-war levels as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz resumes, alleviating supply worries. Following a surge past $126, oil is now hovering around $70. Improved shipping and potential Iranian exports have eased fears of disruptions, leading to a significant price drop. Traders are now anticipating a surplus as physical crude trades at discounts.

content: Goods carrier vehicle driver fled the scene after the collision without stopping or offering assistance

content: Currently, 67 persons – 64 women and three men – are undergoing treatment. Of them, 16 patients are on ventilator support, 21 are receiving nasal oxygen, and 30 patients are stable

content: The diversions will be in effect from 11 a.m. to 5.30 p.m. within the limits of the Ashok Nagar Traffic Police Station

title: Climate change could transform Tamil Nadu’s Western Ghats by 2050 | Focus Tamil Nadu
content:
The Western Ghats may still be green in the coming decades—but they may not be the same forests we know today.

A new study by researchers from Anna University’s Centre for Climate Change and Disaster Management warns that climate change could gradually alter the composition of forests across Tamil Nadu’s Western Ghats, including the Nilgiris, Kodaikanal, Valparai, Courtallam and Kanyakumari regions.

The study suggests that evergreen and deciduous forests could shrink, while drier thorn forests may expand by 2050. Such changes could affect biodiversity, water security, carbon storage and ecosystem stability across the State.

In this episode of The Hindu Focus Tamil Nadu, D. Suresh Kumar explains what the findings mean, why scientists are concerned, and what the future could hold for one of India’s most important biodiversity hotspots.

title: Mehbooba Mufti attends Pandit ceremony to mend broken bonds between communities in Kashmir
content: Ms. Mufti’s move to attend the ceremony has come at a time when many displaced Pandits groups are mulling to return to Kashmir in the wake of the improvement in security scenario and a dip in militant recruitment

title: Discovery of new moth genus in Kerala’s Idukki highlights Western Ghats’ hidden biodiversity
content: Known only from Idukki, the species of tiger moth, named Antaram idukki, underscores the need to conserve biodiversity-rich habitats in Western Ghats, say researchers

title: Explained | Why NMC is phasing out PG diploma medical courses from 2027
content: The National Medical Commission has directed medical colleges offering PG diploma courses to apply for conversion of those seats into MD/MS broad-specialty degree seats; this move is the culmination of a policy shift that has been under way for nearly two decades

content: Mr. Modi also congratulated Mr. Pashinyan on the victory of his Civil Contract Party in the country's parliamentary elections and said India looks forward to working closely together to take India-Armenia relations to new heights Mr.

title: Rahul Gandhi expresses regret in defamation case filed by Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son
content: Kartikeya Singh has filed a defamation complaint in a Bhopal court against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha. Gandhi's application, filed by his lawyer, said that his statement was not related to Singh.

title: Karnataka High Court allows hysterectomy for 23-year-old suffering from intellectual and development disabilities
content: In a rare instance, the High Court of Karnataka has permitted a 23-year-old woman with severe intellectual and development disabilities to undergo total abdominal hysterectomy, ruling that the procedure is in her ‘best interests’ and essential for her health and dignity

content: Priyank Kharge said the upgrades were necessary to strengthen digital administration system and increase its capacity, which will also allow speedy delivery of services without technical glitches

content: The PAC was meeting to deliberate the CAG’s report on the ‘establishment and functioning of Andaman and Nicobar Islands Institute of Medical Sciences’

title: A black market for ‘fake patents’ is a new poison in Indian research
content: Researchers have reported some firms selling U.K. design registrations to Indian academics to artificially boost institutional rankings and individual CVs. These registrations, often granted in 11 days without novelty checks, are misrepresented as international patents despite lacking original research or physical prototypes

content: The Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Rules, 2026, source their legality from the Centre’s argument in the Supreme Court that the freedom to form associations do not include a right to receive unbridled foreign funds or to use the money outside ‘permissible activities’

content: While travelling abroad, passport attests to your nationality, yet it is not a document of your citizenship, says senior official, responding to a query on using a passport to challenge voter exclusion under SIR

content: Saraogi said that under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the BJP remains steadfast in its commitment to democratic values and constitutional principles

content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said that it would be 'unacceptable' to him if the Iran deal included any kind of fees on shipping or maritime activities in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Passport just a travel document, not citizenship proof: MEA
content: the passport attests the nationality of Indians when they are abroad but it isn't a document of citizenship, officials clarified in response to a query . this set off a raging discussion on X, with users asking what then counted as a conclusive proof of citizenship .
